note: Wheelchair accessible - you can roll across the East (city-facing) sidewalk free of charge during daylight; Welcome Center and vista points accessible. Parking very limited, arrive by Muni/accessible rideshare.

The ferry is wheelchair accessible with boarding ramps, but the island is very steep; a free SEAT tram assists up to the cellhouse. Electric chairs manage; manual users likely need assistance. Book well ahead.
Level, accessible waterfront with ramps; sea lions at Pier 39, shops and food. Some sections are wooden boardwalk (a bit bumpy) and it gets crowded. Fun for families.
Large park; JFK Drive is car-free with disabled-only parking lots, paved and relaxed for wheels/strollers. De Young, Academy of Sciences, Japanese Tea Garden nearby. Great family day.

The iconic cable cars are NOT wheelchair accessible (steps, no lift). For accessible transit use Muni buses/streetcars and BART, which are fully accessible. See the F-line historic streetcar as an alternative.
Historic landmark food hall on the Embarcadero; flat, spacious and fully accessible, plenty of room to move. Accessible ferries to Sausalito/Alcatraz depart nearby. Family-friendly.
Narrated bay cruise under the Golden Gate and around Alcatraz; the enclosed lower deck is wheelchair accessible with accessible restrooms (upper open deck is stairs-only). Relaxed and scenic.
The famously crooked street is on a very steep hill; you can view it but the surrounding Russian Hill grade is hard for manual chairs/strollers. Photo stop more than a roll.
North America's oldest Chinatown; the main flat stretches are navigable but many surrounding blocks are hilly and sidewalks get crowded and tight. Great food and atmosphere.
Flat hard-packed shoreline trail with Golden Gate Bridge views, picnic spots and the Warming Hut cafe; easy strolling/rolling from Marina Green to Fort Point. Lovely and relaxed.
